title: Global Asset Manager – Understanding Long-Term Currency Misalignments in Asia
gist: A case study in which Capital Economics analyzed real-exchange-rate misalignments across key Asian economies for a global EM-focused asset manager, building a framework to separate temporary distortions from structural drivers and delivering 30-slide reports per currency.

insights.claim: Persistent misalignments in real exchange rates exist across several Asian currencies, yet their underlying causes are often poorly understood even by sophisticated global asset managers.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Structural FX mispricing in Asian EM is an under-analyzed edge; investors who can decompose it may find durable valuation opportunities.
insights.quote: Despite apparent misalignments in real exchange rates across several Asian currencies, the causes were not well understood.

insights.claim: A robust FX-valuation framework must differentiate temporary distortions from structural factors and benchmark regional trends against global norms.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Distinguishing transient noise from structural drivers is the core discipline for timing and sizing EM currency positions.
insights.quote: differentiate between temporary distortions and structural factors, and benchmark regional trends against global norms

insights.claim: Drivers of Asian real-exchange-rate misalignment include data quality issues, cross-border labour dynamics, housing subsidies, monetary policy regimes, trade and capital flows, and pricing anomalies.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: A checklist of candidate structural drivers to interrogate when a currency screens as cheap or rich versus fair value.
insights.quote: These included data quality issues, cross-border labour dynamics, housing subsidies, monetary policy regimes, trade and capital flows, and pricing anomalies.

insights.claim: Currency valuation plays a pivotal role in long-term EM investment strategy, making structural FX misalignment a key input to portfolio positioning.
insights.speaker: Capital Economics
insights.so_what: Frames FX not as a hedging afterthought but as a primary return driver in EM allocation decisions.
insights.quote: With currency valuation playing a pivotal role in long-term investment strategy
